summary: Point the right buttons at the two external Arive (my1003app/LendWize) forms, add an Apply button to the Get-a-Mortgage dropdown, and replicate the old RankMath redirects — WITHOUT clobbering real pages on the new site.

button_routing.where: Header 'See My Options' button (global, in Header.astro)
button_routing.note: Lightweight inquiry entry. SHARED COMPONENT — coordinate with calculator partner before editing Header.astro.

button_routing.where: Hero 'See My Options' button on every page under the 'Get a Mortgage' nav section (loan-type pages where the visitor already knows what they need)
button_routing.note: Claude Code identifies these pages from the live nav tree. Visitor is ready to apply -> full application.

button_routing.where: 'See My Options' button on the /same-day-approval/ page itself
button_routing.note: By the time they're on this page and click, they're ready for the full app.

button_routing.where: NEW subtle 'Apply for Mortgage' button in the Header 'Get a Mortgage' dropdown
button_routing.note: Place where most appropriate within the dropdown. SHARED COMPONENT — coordinate before editing Header.astro.

redirects.principle: Replicate old RankMath redirects ONLY for slugs that are NOT real pages on the new site. NEVER redirect a slug that renders a real page (that would make the page unreachable).
known_real_pages_keep: /same-day-approval/  (real editorial page on new site — DO NOT redirect; its button links to Arive)
candidate_redirect_sources_from_old_wp: /apply-for-mortgage/   (was a WP post, likely a redirect shell -> full_application_apply)
/purchase-inquiry/     (CONFIRM: real page on new site? If yes, keep + button. If no page, 301 -> inquiry_same_day_purchase)
redirects.safety_rule_for_claude_code: For each candidate source: if a real page file exists in src/pages for that slug, DO NOT add a redirect — instead update that page's CTA button to point at the right Arive URL. Only add a vercel.json 301 for slugs that have NO corresponding page on the new site.

compliance.note: External Arive links — no compliance text needed on the link itself. Arive/NEXA handles the application-side disclosures. Keep button labels as 'See My Options' / 'Apply for Mortgage' (never 'Talk to Jim').
